questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: The 'International Reference Classification of Occupations (ISCO)', which was recently part of a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) between India and the ILO, is best described as:

options.text: A legal framework for the protection of workers in the platform economy.
A statistical system that classifies jobs based on tasks and duties performed.
A global registry for the repatriation of migrant workers during health emergencies.
A mandatory wage-indexation tool for member states of the Global Coalition for Social Justice.

questions.explanation: ISCO is a statistical system that classifies jobs into well-defined groups based on the tasks and duties performed. It is used for research and decision-making. The Indian government signed an MoU with the ILO to promote this classification.
questions.takeaway: ISCO is a classification tool for jobs and tasks, not a legal or regulatory framework for wages.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Indian Government has signed MoU with ILO to promote the International Reference Classification of Occupations (ISCO). ISCO is a statistical system that classifies jobs into well-defined groups based on the tasks and duties performed.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: In the context of international organizations, how does the International Organisation for Mediation (IOMed) differ from the International Court of Justice (ICJ)?

options.text: IOMed is a specialized agency of the United Nations, whereas the ICJ is an independent judicial body.
IOMed focuses on mediation for state and non-state disputes, while the ICJ primarily handles adversarial litigation between states.
IOMed membership is restricted to G20 nations, whereas the ICJ is open to all UN member states.
IOMed was established by the Treaty of Versailles, while the ICJ was established by the China-led initiative.

questions.explanation: IOMed is a China-led initiative established as an alternative to the ICJ/PCA, focusing on mediation. Its scope includes disputes between states, between a state and foreign nationals, and commercial disagreements. The ICJ is the principal judicial organ of the UN and primarily deals with legal disputes between states (litigation).
questions.takeaway: IOMed is a mediation-focused alternative to traditional litigation-heavy international courts.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: China formally established the IOMed as a global alternative to traditional institutions such as the International Court of Justice (ICJ) and the Permanent Court of Arbitration. ... Purpose: Dedicated to resolving international disputes through mediation. ... Scope: Disputes between states, Disputes Between a state and nationals of another country, International commercial disagreements.
